title: xylophacos stipularis
intro: Xylophacos stipularis, also known as the "stick-tipped Xylophacos," is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Xylophacos stipularis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Xylophacos stipularis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
